channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Colorectal cancer is leading cancer killer for people under 50
item.description: James Van Der Beek's death at age 48 highlights a sad statistic: More people under 50 are getting colorectal cancer.

item.title: A telephone-based intervention reduced loneliness and improved well-being among older adults
item.description: 1. Four weeks of telephone-delivered behavioral activation and mindfulness interventions by lay counsellors were associated with reduced loneliness an...

item.title: RFK Jr. made promises to get his job as health secretary. He's broken many of them
item.description: In his confirmation hearings, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. told U.S. senators that he would not cut funding for vaccine research or change the nation's offic...

item.title: Judge blocks Trump admin from rescinding health grants to Democratic-led states
item.description: The ruling temporarily blocks the Trump Administration from cutting $600 million in public health grants that had already been allocated to four Democ...

item.title: Northern Queensland faces rising mosquito-borne virus threat
item.description: New research led by James Cook University scientists shows Northern Queensland is facing an escalating public health threat from mosquito-borne virus ...

item.title: New research finds data-driven staffing model delivers major cost savings for health care systems
item.description: New research published in Operations Research shows that health care systems can substantially reduce overtime, idle time, and overall staffing costs ...

item.title: Advanced Therapies Show Similar Safety in Crohn’s Disease
item.description: Advanced treatments for Crohn’s disease show comparable safety profiles, with no meaningful differences in risks for serious infections or major cardi...

item.title: Colorectal cancer is increasing among young people, James Van Der Beek's death reminds
item.description: An increasing number of people are dying of colorectal cancer at a young age, including those as young as 20. Actor James Van Der Beek, who was diagno...

item.title: Alcohol Counseling After US Dietary Guidelines Update
item.description: New US dietary guidelines may raise confusion for patients with chronic kidney disease. Experts suggest approaches for counseling. Medscape Medical Ne...

item.title: Sour grapes? Experience of sour food depends on individual consumer
item.description: Biting into a tart green apple is a different taste and sensory experience than sucking juice from a lemon—and both significantly vary from accidental...

item.title: World-first toolkit puts autistic lens on suicide prevention
item.description: Researchers from La Trobe University have launched a world-leading toolkit for health care professionals to better identify and support Autistic adult...

item.title: H Pylori Eradication Cuts Gastric Cancer Risk
item.description: Researchers say it’s never too late to eradicate Helicobacter pylori infection, as new evidence shows survival benefits across all adult age groups. M...

item.title: About 1 in 15 Older ED Patients Receive High-Risk Meds
item.description: Older adults discharged from the ED are frequently prescribed potentially inappropriate medications that geriatric ED guidelines recommend avoiding, a...

item.title: ‘Penisgate’: Hyaluronic Acid and Olympic Controversy
item.description: Hyaluronic acid injections have been alleged to alter ski jump suit fit and confer an aerodynamic edge. Physicians should be aware of the procedure, r...

item.title: Propofol is associated with lower mortality in sepsis-associated encephalopathy
item.description: 1. Among patients with sepsis-associated encephalopathy, propofol monotherapy was associated with lower one-year all-cause mortality compared to no se...

item.title: Olympic athletes push their bodies to the limit. Should we?
item.description: Elite athletes often push through pain to achieve victory. But, everyday exercisers need to distinguish between soreness which is normal and pain whic...

item.title: Court rebuffs plea from domestic workers for better pay and respect
item.description: They're often paid low wages and lack job protections. A petition to the country's supreme court to support their demands did not see success — and th...

item.title: Antimicrobial resistance causes: Why social factors matter more than drugs
item.description: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) is being talked about more now than ever before, and for good reason. When medicines stop working for common infections...

item.title: The medical referral process: Why it fails and how to fix it
item.description: For clinicians, trainees, and health system leaders, the referral process is supposed to be a bridge connecting patients to the specialty care they ne...

item.title: Physician wellness theater: Why pizza parties do not fix burnout
item.description: There are resilience workshops. Mindfulness apps. Yoga sessions scheduled between clinics. Burnout surveys. Pizza in the call room on Doctor’s Day. Th...

item.title: Executive order on homelessness: Why forced treatment fails

item.title: Immigrant caregiver burden: the hidden cost of the five-year Medicaid wait

item.title: Polypharmacy is associated with higher risk of mortality and hospitalizations among older adults
item.description: 1. Polypharmacy was associated with a higher risk of mortality and hospitalization at one-year and five-years among older adults aged >75 years. Evide...

item.title: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine®: Daily caffeine intake linked to lower dementia risk, AAP sues over federal vaccine schedule overhaul, Catherine O’Hara passes away after short illness, and GLP-1 receptor agonists reduce endometrial cancer risk
item.description: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine® is a pop-culture medical newsletter and exclusive benefit for 2 Minute Medicine Plus subscribers. Moderate caffeine int...

item.title: Oral nonstatin medication significantly lowers cholesterol in high-risk patients
item.description: 1. In this randomized controlled trial, once-daily oral enlicitide was associated with sustained reductions in low-density lipoprotein cholesterol lev...
